user_message: I need the team view on migration architecture. I think there's an opportunity here but need to validate.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sandra Taylor <sandra.taylor@northwindtra.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-27 08:20 EST
Subject: migration architecture - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about migration architecture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Sandra Taylor <sandra.taylor@northwindtra.com>
Date: 2024-02-10 13:28 EST
Subject: Follow-up: migration architecture Review

Just checking in on the migration architecture proposal. Our Head of Digital is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Sandra Taylor <sandra.taylor@northwindtra.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-24 15:04 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Northwind Traders migration architecture

Our team encountered some challenges with migration architecture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - Northwind Traders
Date: 2024-03-28
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), Michelle (Northwind Traders)

[00:01:30] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into migration architecture.

[00:05:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:07:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

[00:09:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:11:00] Michelle (Northwind Traders): Let me add some context -- we've been working on performance requirements and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:14:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:19:00] Michelle (Northwind Traders): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Pre-Meeting Brief template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
